name: Achēv
address: 263 Queen Street East, Unit 14, Brampton, Ontario L6W WK6
description:
I had a very positive experience with Marilyn Afano as an instructor during my dad’s English assessment. My dad can sometimes be a bit test-anxious and unsure of himself when it comes to language assessments, but Marilyn created such a supportive and comfortable environment that it immediately put him at ease.

What really stood out was how clearly and patiently she explained everything. She didn’t just give instructions quickly and move on—she took the time to go through each part of the assessment step by step, making sure my dad fully understood what was expected. Her explanations were very detailed, which was incredibly helpful for someone who can feel nervous or overwhelmed during testing situations.

She also demonstrated a lot of patience and professionalism. If my dad needed clarification, she was more than willing to explain things again without making him feel rushed or pressured. That level of understanding makes a huge difference for people who may struggle with confidence during language assessments.

Overall, Marilyn Afano is an amazing instructor. She is knowledgeable, patient, and genuinely cares about helping people understand the process. Her ability to communicate clearly and supportively makes the assessment experience much less stressful. I would highly recommend her to anyone who is taking an English assessment and wants someone who truly takes the time to guide and support them.

I would like to thank Assessor Marilyn Alfano for the assessment. The process was clear, fair, and professional. The instructions were easy to understand, and helpful feedback was given.

The assessor was kind, supportive, and knowledgeable. This assessment helped me understand the registration requirements better and supported me in moving forward toward registration.

Thank you for your support and guidance.

Today’s session was very interesting, and I gained valuable insights that I can apply in my life. It will help me build better connections with the Aboriginal community and benefit me both personally and professionally. I would like to attend similar webinars in the future, especially those that focus on understanding the diverse cultures of the Canadian community. Please let me know if any are scheduled. Thank you once again!

A very nice place for newcomers to the country with several useful and free resources available in relation to education and employment.  The staff also is extremely helpful and polite and always ready to help you. I had a very nice experience with Ms Jasleen Kaur and Mrs Kumari who were always around to help everyone with their professional expertise, knowledge with a very nice and helpful attitude.

I wanted to share my feedback on my recent English test at Achev Agency. The agency is very professional and organized, and Marilyn Alfano, who attended me, was kind, helpful, and made the experience smooth and comfortable. I truly appreciate their support.

name: University Settlement
address: 23 Grange Road, Toronto, Ontario M5T 1C3
description:
We come here for swimming and music lessons for our 6 year old daughter. She thoroughly enjoys the classes. Especially the music class.

We like that the location isn’t too far from where we live and that we can walk here. People also just cannot walk in without being buzzed into the building. The change rooms also have a code to prevent unauthorized entry.

I haven’t dealt with the staff personally as my wife deals with them. From what I’ve seen, they are fairly helpful.

Admin staff Renee is very kind and helpful. She answered efficiently about my enquiry. My little daughter Joyce was taken care very well during after school daycare. Highly recommended that.

Pool is closed it seems like 25% of the time. Some months it’s 50%. And if the pool is open often there is no instructor. Communication is spotty at best.

So grateful for all the support our family has received at this incredibly diverse daycare! In the three years my son has been there, we’ve never had any issues. Everyone is very loving, respectful, and caring.

Front desk staff are lovely and fitness room is quiet. However, I won't renew my membership mainly due to hygiene concerns.

I often need to bring empty spray bottles to a staff member to refill, or request paper towels for wiping gym equipment. Haven't had hand sanitizer (fitness room) for months. So, little to no sanitizing before or after equipment use. Unfortunately, most members don't bother - I see visible sweat left on benches/mats that don't get wiped. I've asked for support with messaging but no luck. Meanwhile, I receive countless emails about pool closures and cancelled classes.

I want to like it here and in many ways I do. But they need to drastically lower membership fees for what is offered and ensure basic courtesy and hygiene is practiced. A good start would be supplying members with what is needed to do so.

Went to drop off a cash donation for the food bank and was greeted warmly by the lady behind the glass.

Very disappointing first trip to the centre. I arrived for an Early On drop-in playgroup 15 minutes after the start time posted on the website, and was told by the person at the desk that the group was full for the day. When I asked her to explain, she said that it was first come, first served, and that there is a max of 30 or 35 people. I can totally understand these restrictions, however they were not posted anywhere on their website (as, in my opinion, they should have been). Had I been aware of them, I would have arrived before the start time to make sure my kids got a spot. As it was, I had a very disappointed 2 and 4 year old. A very frustrating experience for everyone…

I've primarily used the EarlyON program here. As a first-time mom new to this neighborhood, EarlyON has been invaluable in providing the resources and support I need. The staff are passionate and highly knowledgeable about children's development and family resources, offering a high-quality program that includes playdates, seminars, and a toy library. Though the playgroups can fill up quickly, it's a testament to the program's excellence. I'm incredibly grateful for access to these amazing programs and such dedicated staff.

name: YMCA of Greater Toronto
address: 101 YMCA Blvd
description:
I absolutely loved this sports center — until one particular incident. One evening, when I came to the pool, a girl warned me to be careful because someone was watching from the area in the women’s locker room reserved for people with disabilities. I didn’t pay much attention at first and went swimming. But when I came back, I noticed the same person still standing there, just watching.

I immediately called the staff, who were friendly but, unfortunately, far too tolerant of this voyeur. I understand that under Canadian law people can’t be physically removed without process, but this man had been standing there for over an hour watching women change, and the staff handled it inappropriately — even giving him “five more minutes” instead of putting women’s SAFETY first.

Will I come back? Yes, because I truly love this center and the variety of activities it offers. But I strongly want to see SAFETY prioritized above everything else.

Pros: an incredible variety of sports, very friendly staff.
Cons: SAFETY is seriously lacking.

I love the place and i’ve seen so many comments on people at the gym and how crowded it can be. Be realistic, guys. You get what you pay and I can say that at other gyms you pay more to attend all these classes… 40cad per class, here its unlimited classes you can join in a month!

I love the variety of classes we have here. Yes, there are lots of people, all ages, all backgrounds, making it a bit challenging to have a private space; just see this as an opportunity to practice your patience and respect to all.

Staffs are very nice. They all try to help. Just speak to them and ask politely. We are all human here.

Last thing, if you cant change the environment and other people, change your view of things. If you are firmed of who you are and are not willing to adapt or compromise, then change the environment :) No one holds you back.

Hi everyone, and Happy New Year.

I’ve been a member of this Central YMCA since last year, and I genuinely love this place and the people here. I’m writing this review in response to Chayapat’s review, not to dismiss it, but to add an important perspective.

Many of her points are valid. Where I disagree is with the idea that the solution is simply to leave if the environment isn’t ideal.

If we truly care about a place, shouldn’t the question be how we can make it better, especially when moving elsewhere isn’t always an option? Not everyone can just go to another gym, another neighbourhood, or another city.

Constructive feedback is how communities improve. Accepting whatever is provided without question, simply because we’ve paid, discourages critical thinking and meaningful change.

We should be free to raise concerns just as much as we are free to offer compliments, even when we don’t all agree. That’s how a shared space grows stronger.

Thank you YMCA!

I’ve loved this gym for years because of the diverse group of people who come here. But lately, the customer service just hasn’t been the same. There are usually 2–3 people at the front desk, and if you forget your card, one staff member especially gives you a hard time about it — even about my kids’ cards.

If the goal is to make sure no one forgets their card, maybe there could be an option to save it on your phone, like a Presto card.

Also, the gym is supposed to close at 10, but they start asking people to leave around 9:35. One time, someone even turned off the lights while I was finishing my run — I had less than 1 km left, which would’ve taken under five minutes.

Honestly, it’s making me think about checking out other gyms. At Cooper, they at least come around at 9:45 and give everyone a proper 15-minute heads-up before closing.

Hi Team,

I’m writing to formally raise a complaint regarding the ongoing conduct of a group of approximately six members who regularly use the badminton courts during peak hours.

There have been repeated incidents where these individuals refuse to yield or rotate courts, monopolize corner courts for extended periods, and exclude other members from play. On multiple occasions, they have made dismissive and bullying remarks toward other members, including comments about skill level and statements that they do not want to “waste expensive birdies” on others. Members have been told to move to other courts so this group can continue playing privately during prime time.

This behaviour has been witnessed by myself and all other members on multiple occasions. These individuals attend regularly during badminton hours, and their conduct has affected many players. I am also aware that multiple complaints have already been raised by other members and guests. Unfortunately, despite this, the behaviour has continued without any visible change, accountability, or apology.

This conduct directly contradicts the YMCA’s values of respect, inclusion, and community. It creates an unwelcoming environment and negatively impacts members’ sense of safety and enjoyment, particularly for newer and female players.

I am requesting that YMCA management formally address this issue and clarify what steps will be taken to prevent further harassment, bullying, and court monopolization. Please also advise on the appropriate process for reporting and resolving these incidents going forward.

I believe in the YMCA community and would like to see its values actively upheld. I look forward to your response and to a constructive resolution.

Thank you for your time and attention.

Kind regards,
Hongmin Park

name: Ausome Karma Services
address: 16 Dunsmore Gardens, North York, ON, M3H 3M2
description:
Outstanding life coach. Very skilled with people with ASD. He is supportive, structured and truly effective. He helps build confidence and real life skills. My son had made huge improvement in his self esteem and self confidence.  Highly recommend.

Shawn Kendal of Ausome Karma Services is an excellent group facilitator and coach! My son, who on the ASD spectrum, has benefited greatly from Mr. Kendal's one-on-one coaching and attending his groups. He creates an inclusive, safe, interactive space for people with neurodevelopmental differences or neurodivergent youth to come together and informally practice social skills while doing awesome activities such as going to museums, hikes, going to baseball games, plays, and other events all across GTA. Shawn Kendal is kind, and comfortable to connect with for the kids/youth he coaches while maintaining professionalism, reliability, and responsibility as a facilitator. I am very grateful for Shawn Kendal and his programs, as they have provided many fun, educational, and social opportunities for my son over the years. Highly recommend!

My 19-year old son has been attending for a few years now the outings that Shawn has organized with other youths.  I would say that no other person outside his family has had such a positive influence on my son as Shawn.  I personally attended the first outing a few years ago and have communicated regularly with Shawn about all the necessary details to make sure my son could cope with and enjoy the outings.  Very few people I have ever met, including seasoned professionals, have the demeanour, compassion, patience and skills that my son benefits from each week.  I saw this firsthand when about five or six youths were all busy trying to deal with ordering their lunches at a fast food restaurant. It's quite marvellous to watch him cooly and calmly treat each person and whatever might be the challenge of the moment, listening and helping the youths through the experience.
My son has developed from a boy with major challenges even leaving the house, to eventually be a young man who leaves on his own, takes TTC to Shawn's, cares about and takes charge of managing his own health, and enjoys the process of meeting others and making a meaningful contribution to society at a foodbank once a week. He trusts Shawn, and Shawn backs up a lot of what I've been trying to teach my son about what it takes to  move toward self-sufficiency and the bigger world out there.  The decision to send my son to Shawn's activities is the single best decision I've made for my son since he was born.

Shawn is an amazing, kind life coach. He treats the campers like family and truly cares about them.
Shawn is patient, empatheic. My son had been attending at his camp for over 3 years now, we are so happy and lucky to have Shawn in my son's life. Thank you Shawn.

My son has attended many programs through Ausome Karma and the experience has been wonderful.  All activities planned are fun and engaging.  The group goes on great field trips and participate in community outings.  My son enjoys all the planned activities and feels welcome in the group.

address: 1349 Western Road, London, Ontario, N6G 1H3, Canada
description:
Always amazing and i tresting been there  so.many times and still.discover amazing things. Also the buffeet at huron is crazy!!!!! $19 to stuff your face is unbeatable. Fyi u need to be a student

As an International student at Huron, I have to say, it has been an incredible experience. It was really tough moving away from my family to a new country where I knew no one. But Huron supported me so well through my transition. The small class sizes helped me connect with my professors and get the extra help I needed. The university and student council are constantly organizing events, making campus a lively and entertaining place to be. And the best part: I will always find someone I know in the Beaver Dam or in the Library or just walking between classes.

Huron is a fantastic school for any student who is seeking a more intentional academic experience. Small class sizes with professors and faculty who prioritize the well-being and learning of their students results in growth personally, professionally, and intellectually. Huron is a truly unique place.

Overall this is probably one of the best places one can choose to study. It has a nice campus, good quality administration, and good professors. There is potential for scholarships and the bursaries can be very generous when needed. There is great prospect for employment within the school or externally via exclusive internships. There is nothing more one could ask for honestly, I've just finished my 4th year and have nothing but good things to say about this school.

name: George Brown- College Vocational Program
address: 341 King Street East, Toronto, ON, M5A 1L1
description:
I have studied at this college for one year. The weakness of this college lies in its lack of a cohesive administrative system. For even a minor issue, I had to navigate various departments of the college for over a month, passing my matter from one department to another in a closed loop, without resolving the problem.

Another weakness of this college is the imbalance between number of students in classes. In one semester, I had a class with 27 students, while in another class at the same level, there was a class with only 8 students. When I approached the office and raised this issue, after a week, the college responded that, from their perspective, the classes are balanced, and I cannot object.

Don't waste your time. They don't respond to emails or calls!

I applied multiple times and was repeatedly denied admission despite meeting all the stated requirements. I experienced significant difficulties in communicating with the admissions office or departments. Emails and phone calls went unanswered, leaving me feeling ignored and frustrated. The admissions process appears to be unclear and inconsistent. I observed the college admitting new students while my applications were repeatedly denied, despite seemingly identical qualifications. This lack of transparency and communication has wasted a significant amount of my time and caused considerable stress. I am deeply disappointed with my experience and the lack of respect shown by the college towards prospective students. I recommend that the college improve its communication channels and provide clearer and more consistent information regarding the admissions process. I urge prospective students to carefully consider their options before applying to this college.

ESL department management is bloody poor.
They send us an offer and agreement and we  accept it and pay the full amount on time then they change their mind and rules at the end of the month which means you don’t have time apply to other universities.

Me and my wife got shocked and we were so disappointed  , she got depressed because your bad management.

You are not allowed to change the already accepted and paid offer.  So disappointed ☹️

I attended this school for one year and two months, starting at Level 2 and reaching Level 9. It was an exceptional year during which I significantly improved my language skills. Each teacher who instructed me—Johanna, David, Suhaila, Ola, Mary, Michael, and Deborah—was highly skilled in teaching, and I will never forget the excellence of the volunteer programs led by Dorothy and her team. I feel very grateful to this college and these professors.

Their ESL program sucks. The majority of instructors are not English native speakers, and these instructors need to attend accent reduction courses themselves indeed.
On 3B level, they have changed the instructors 3 times so far, and they keep changing them. Their IT team is like 20 years ago and not updated recently.
I do not recommend taking any English program in this college.
